steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content:
Sentence 1 defines post-consumer waste (waste after reaching the consumer). The blank introduces a sentence about pre-consumer waste (waste before reaching the consumer). These are opposites — the transition must signal contrast.
  • Choice A — By contrast,: Signals direct opposition between the two types of waste, which is exactly the relationship the passage establishes. ✓
  • Choice B — For example,: Signals that what follows exemplifies the previous sentence; pre-consumer waste is not an example of post-consumer waste. ✗
  • Choice C — As a result,: Signals causation — that pre-consumer waste somehow results from the catalog being post-consumer waste, which is illogical. ✗
  • Choice D — Specifically,: Signals elaboration or precision about the preceding idea; this sentence introduces a contrasting category, not a clarifying detail about post-consumer waste. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the contrast
steps.content: Post-consumer (after) and pre-consumer (before) are opposite points on a timeline. Only a contrast transition fits this structure. That matches Choice A — By contrast,.
